title: Ideologies of political parties
description: Explain how ideologies of the two major parties shape policy debates.
topic_path: How do the major political parties differ in ideology?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of typical conservative and liberal views?**

 | Liberal | Conservative
- | - | -
**A** | More likely to vote with the Democratic party | More likely to vote with the Republican party
**B** | Likely to support students' right to engage in voluntary prayer in public schools | Likely to oppose public schools facilitating voluntary prayer at school events
**C** | Less likely to believe the death penalty is cruel and unusual punishment  | More likely to believe the death penalty is cruel and unusual punishment
**D** | Likely to favor higher military spending by the federal government | Likely to favor reduced military spending by the federal government
[[☃ radio 1]]

** Conservative ideology is best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A belief in limiting government regulation of the economy, but upholding traditional values by imposing restrictions on behaviors like same-sex marriage
A belief in increasing the  role of government to regulate the economy and create government programs to help the most needy
A belief in the superiority of the United States over other countries and seeks to promote the interests of the United States, even when it hurts other countries
A belief in emphasizing the needs of communities over the rights of the individual

assessment_items.question_content:
**Liberal ideology is best defined as which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A belief in increasing the role of government to regulate the economy and creating government programs to help the most needy
A belief in limiting government interference in personal, social, and economic issues
A belief in limiting government regulation of the economy, but upholding traditional values by imposing restrictions on behaviors like same-sex marriage
A belief in the superiority of the United States over other countries and the need to promote the interests of the United States, even if doing so hurts other countries

assessment_items.question_content:
** Which of the following groups would be most likely to support a constitutional amendment that bans affirmative action programs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

** Which of the following groups would be most likely to support a constitutional amendment banning the use of the death penalty? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

** Which of the following groups would be most likely to support a piece of legislation that lowers the corporate tax rate? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the text below to answer the following question. 

> “Simplifying our tax code will help the middle class because it will make it easier for small businesses to hire and grow. And we agree with the President that we should lower our corporate tax rate, which is one of the highest in the world, so that companies will start bringing their money and their jobs back here from overseas.”

>Senator Marco Rubio in a response to the State of the Union Address, 2013

** Which of the following ideological perspectives is most consistent with the author?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the text below to answer the following question. 

> “We should accept our responsibility in the fight against global warming by supporting stricter pollution emission standards for cars sold in Maryland by adopting the Clean Cars Act. By taking action, we are not only going to be able to help children who suffer everyday from asthma, we are also going to be able to remove pollutants from the air, and be able, to a degree, to remove those pollutants from the Bay. We can join 11 other states in getting this done, and getting it done this year.”

> Governor Martin O’Malley of Maryland, *State of the State Address,* 2007


**Which of the following ideological perspectives is most consistent with the author?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the text below to answer the following question. 

> “Above all, the role of policymakers must be to lift government-imposed barriers to stronger communities and flourishing lives. Fiscal responsibility and economic opportunity are but means to a more critical end: the rebuilding of broken communities and the empowerment of families and citizens. The ever-expansive activism of the federal government drains the vitality and displaces the primacy of the bedrock institutions that define America.”

>Congressman Paul Ryan, *The Path to Prosperity*, 2012


** Which of the following ideological perspectives is most consistent with the author?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the infographic below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f2efaee0a02b7c840eab9fde17251e3.svg)

**Which of the following ideological perspectives is most consistent with the Republican Party’s platform?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Use the infographic below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f2efaee0a02b7c840eab9fde17251e3.svg)

**Which of the following ideological perspectives is most consistent with the Democratic Party’s platform?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

**Which of the following is an accurate comparison of typical conservative and liberal views concerning the role of government?**

 | Liberal | Conservative
- | - | -
**A** | State governments should have the right to enact anti-union laws  | State governments should not infringe on an individual’s right to collectively organize
**B** | The federal government should strengthen background checks for gun ownership | The federal government should not create laws that infringe on an individual’s right to bear arms
**C** | The federal government should protect an unborn child’s right to life  | The federal government should have no role in an individual’s right have an abortion
**D** | State governments should have sole ownership of the curriculum taught in public schools | State governments should have their curriculums reviewed by the federal Department of Education 

[[☃ radio 1]]
